revision.text:
[[de:Ipip-Fest]] '''Ipip Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W3-f-F35-n-M17-Q3-t:Z4 U36-t:Z4 I12 Spelling during the New Kingdom period, see Leitz: ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen.'' Vol. 1: ''3 bis y.'' Leuven et al. 2002, p. 219. |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb.f-nefer-en-Ipet-hemet-s
''ḥbf-nfr-n-Jpt-ḥmt.s'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Beautiful Feast for {{ill|Ipet|de|Ipet|ja|イペト|zh|伊佩特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1618009|1=Q1618009}} by her Majesty'' |NAME2 = W3-f-F35-n-M17-Q3:Y1 U36-X3-s-G7 Spelling from the {{ill|21st Dynasty|de|21. Dynastie (Ägypten)|ja|第21王朝 (エジプト)|zh|埃及第二十一王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/21st Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q748151|1=Twenty-first Dynasty of Egypt}} to the {{ill|24th Dynasty|de|24. Dynastie (Ägypten)|ja|第24王朝 (エジプト)|zh|埃及第二十四王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24th Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q754749|1=Twenty-fourth Dynasty of Egypt}}, see Christian Leitz: ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen.'' Vol. 1: ''3 bis y.'' Leuven et al. 2002, p. 219. |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb.f-nefer-en-Ipet-hemet-s
''ḥbf-nfr-n-Jpt-ḥmt.s'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Beautiful Feast for Ipet by her Majesty'' }} The '''Ipip Festival''', also '''Apip Festival''', ({{ill|Greek language|en|Greek language|qid=Q9129|lt=Greek|1=Greek language}} '''Epiphi Festival'''; {{ill|Coptic language|en|Coptic language|qid=Q36155|lt=Coptic|1=Coptic language}} '''Epip Festival'''; {{ill|Arabic|en|Arabic|qid=Q13955|lt=Arabic|1=Arabic}} '''Abib Festival''') was celebrated as the "Beautiful Feast for {{ill|Ipet|de|Ipet|ja|イペト|zh|伊佩特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1618009|1=Q1618009}} by her Majesty," associated with the "Union of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|qid=Q760218|lt=Eye of Ra|1=Eye of Ra}} and the {{ill|Eye of Horus|en|Eye of Horus|qid=Q211286|lt=Eye of Horus|1=Eye of Horus}} on the Day of Purification." It began in the {{ill|Old Kingdom|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国時代 (エジプト)|zh|古王國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q177819|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}} as one of the main festivals at {{ill|New moon|en|New moon|qid=Q108566|lt=New Moon|1=New moon}} on the first day of the month {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|ja|イペト・ヘメト月|zh|伊佩特-赫梅特月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet-hemet (month)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1672177|lt=Ipet-hemet|1=Ipet-hemet (month)}}Rolf Krauss: ''Der Kalender des Papyrus Ebers.'' In: ''Sothis- und Monddaten.'' Hildesheim 1985, p. 104, also: ''Das Epiphi-Fest.'' pp. 151–154. of the {{ill|Sothic calendar|de|Sothis-Kalender|ja|ソティス暦|zh|索提斯曆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. Its main cult center was {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|qid=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}}. == Background == [[File:Edfu pylon.jpg|thumb|Pylon of the Horus Temple in Edfu]] The Ipet-hemet festival is attested at Edfu during the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} under {{ill|Senusret I|en|Senusret I|qid=Q18763|lt=Senusret I|1=Senusret I}} and {{ill|Amenemhat II|en|Amenemhat II|qid=Q18734|lt=Amenemhat II|1=Amenemhat II}}. Preparations began in the eleventh month {{ill|Chenti-chet|de|Chenti-chet|ja|ケンティ・ケト|zh|肯蒂-切特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chenti-chet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1070110|1=Khenti-khet}}, later shifted to the tenth month {{ill|Payni|de|Payni|ja|パイニ|zh|佩尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Payni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q643869|1=Paoni}}, while the twelfth month Ipet-hemet moved to the eleventh month Ipip (Epiphi). In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, the goddesses Hathor and {{ill|Mut|en|Mut|qid=Q29409|lt=Mut|1=Mut}} became closely associated, eventually merging under "Mut-Hathor in {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|qid=Q101583|lt=Thebes|1=Thebes, Egypt}}." The cult of Mut moved from {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|qid=Q5715|lt=Memphis|1=Memphis, Egypt}} to {{ill|Karnak|en|Karnak|qid=Q522862|lt=Karnak|1=Karnak}}.{{ill|James Henry Breasted|de|James Henry Breasted|ja|ジェームズ・ヘンリー・ブレステッド|zh|詹姆斯·亨利·布雷斯特德|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/James Henry Breasted|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q365982|1=James Henry Breasted}}: ''Ancient records of Egypt.'' Vol. 2: §622. The rise of Mut-Hathor to "Mistress of Heaven" integrated her as the "Eye of Ra," merging characteristics of {{ill|Sekhmet|en|Sekhmet|qid=Q146104|lt=Sekhmet|1=Sekhmet}}. Thutmose III arranged the festival for the new Hathor temple at Edfu, oriented towards the Horus temple. == Festival Procedure == === Departure from Dendera === [[File:DenderaHathorTempleComplexQenaEgypt517-2007feb10PhotoByCsorfolyDaniel.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Dendera Temple complex|en|Dendera Temple complex|qid=Q735254|lt=Temple of Dendera|1=Dendera Temple complex}}]] Immediately after the full moon of the eleventh month, preparations started in Dendera, followed by the procession on the Nile towards Edfu, stopping at various shrines along the way. === Arrival at Edfu === The Hathor statue first visited her temple, then the Horus temple, symbolically handing over the Eye of Ra. After 14 days, at full moon, the main rituals concluded, and Hathor returned to Dendera. Earlier assumptions that the Ipip Festival involved a {{ill|Hieros gamos|en|Hieros gamos|qid=Q1334402|lt=hierogamy|1=Hieros gamos}} between Amun-Re and Hathor are no longer supported. == See also == * {{ill|Amun-Re festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|アメン・ラー祭|zh|阿蒙-拉節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q481558|1=Amun-Re Festival}} * {{ill|Opet Festival|en|Opet Festival|qid=Q1194158|lt=Opet Festival|1=Opet Festival}} ==References== {{translated page|de|Ipip-Fest|version=234682603|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:イペト祭]] [[de:Ipip-Fest]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1672200}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Schemu III]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Schemu IV]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
